pages.title: Sample
pages.summary: Start with the broad sample context. Published repository workflows appear later as example backends rather than at the start of the wizard.

questions.label: What type of sample are you working with?
questions.description: Choose the broad context first.

options.label: Environmental sample
options.help: Environmental surveillance material such as air, passive water, or eDNA.

options.label: Clinical / isolate
options.help: Purified isolates, barcoded isolate collections, or clinical metagenomes.

options.label: Food safety
options.help: Food-associated enrichment and recovery workflows.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep the route neutral and decide on the next page.

pages.title: Material
pages.summary: Describe the biological material or assay class, not the kit. This keeps the early routing generic and reduces repo-specific bias.

questions.label: What kind of material or assay does this represent?
questions.description: Choose the assay branch that best matches your data.

options.label: Long-read metagenomic DNA
options.help: Environmental shotgun DNA for taxonomic, functional, assembly, or AMR analysis.

options.label: RNA virome material
options.help: Environmental RNA material aimed at untargeted viral discovery.

options.label: eDNA 12S amplicon
options.help: Amplicon-based 12S metabarcoding rather than shotgun metagenomics.

options.label: Single isolate long-read DNA
options.help: Single-isolate long-read sequencing aimed at assembly or AMR interpretation.

options.label: Barcoded isolate long-read DNA
options.help: Multiplexed isolate sequencing where one organism is expected per barcode.

options.label: Clinical metagenome
options.help: Complex clinical material rather than a purified isolate.

options.label: Listeria enrichment / recovery material
options.help: Food-safety material intended for Listeria target recovery and comparison workflows.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep the material class neutral.

pages.title: Target
pages.summary: Choose the main analytical target or deliverable. This is still a generic research question page, not an example-backend page.

questions.label: What do you want to do with the data?
questions.description: Choose one main target or a full-pipeline outcome.

options.label: Taxonomy profiling
options.help: Identify which organisms are present.

options.label: Functional profiling
options.help: Interpret gene content and functional potential.

options.label: MAG recovery
options.help: Recover metagenome-assembled genomes.

options.label: AMR surveillance
options.help: Screen for pathogen and AMR signals in environmental DNA.

options.label: Full environmental pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest environmental DNA action sheet.

options.label: Viral discovery
options.help: Characterize untargeted RNA viral content.

options.label: Full virome pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest RNA virome action sheet.

options.label: Biodiversity metabarcoding
options.help: Use 12S reads for biodiversity inference.

options.label: Host-range inference
options.help: Use 12S reads to infer likely host context from the sample.

options.label: Full amplicon pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest 12S amplicon action sheet.

options.label: Complete genome assembly
options.help: Aim for a high-quality isolate assembly.

options.label: Annotation
options.help: Annotate isolate genome content after assembly.

options.label: Hybrid assembly
options.help: Combine long reads with short reads for isolate assembly.

options.label: AMR / virulence profiling
options.help: Profile AMR and virulence determinants.

options.label: Full isolate pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest isolate action sheet for the chosen route.

options.label: Genome assembly + annotation
options.help: Assemble and annotate barcoded isolates.

options.label: Phylogenetics / outbreak tracking
options.help: Comparative isolate analysis and relatedness.

options.label: Plasmid reconstruction
options.help: Resolve and compare plasmid content.

options.label: AMR host association
options.help: Link AMR genes or plasmids to hosts in a clinical metagenome.

options.label: Full host-association pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest AMR host-association action sheet.

options.label: Listeria target recovery
options.help: Recover and analyze Listeria targets.

options.label: AS vs native comparison
options.help: Compare adaptive sampling against the matched control route.

options.label: Recovered target AMR profiling
options.help: Interpret AMR content after target recovery.

options.label: Full food safety pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest food-safety action sheet.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Keep the target neutral.

pages.title: Published Example Context
pages.summary: Choose the published study context that is closest to your case. Unsupported contexts remain available and will map to the nearest published example backend.

questions.label: Which published study context is closest to your case?
questions.description: These options appear only after the generic route is narrowed.

pages.title: Expert Tuning
pages.summary: These settings refine the recommendation inside the chosen backend. They do not reroute you to a different published example.

questions.label: Library prep kit or run mode
questions.description: Use this to tune demultiplexing, barcode trimming, and ONT-specific guidance.

options.label: LSK114
options.help: Ligation-based single-sample DNA workflow.

options.label: RBK114.24
options.help: Rapid Barcoding Kit 24 V14.

options.label: NBD114.24
options.help: Native Barcoding Kit 24 V14.

options.label: Amplicon workflow
options.help: Amplicon-focused workflow.

options.label: Barcoded metagenome
options.help: Multiplexed metagenomic route rather than isolate-only processing.

options.label: Adaptive sampling
options.help: Real-time enrichment or depletion during sequencing.

options.label: Standard control
options.help: Matched non-adaptive sequencing control.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Leave kit selection neutral.

questions.label: Current input format
questions.description: Use this to tune the documented entry point.

options.label: POD5
options.help: Raw signal data in POD5 format.

options.label: FAST5
options.help: Legacy raw signal data.

options.label: BAM
options.help: Basecalled or aligned BAM.

options.label: FASTQ
options.help: Basecalled long reads.

options.label: Paired-end FASTQ
options.help: Short-read support data, usually Illumina.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Leave input format neutral.

questions.label: Are the data multiplexed?
questions.description: Used to refine early preprocessing and routing assumptions inside the selected backend.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Barcodes are present or multiple samples were pooled.

options.label: No
options.help: Single-sample or already-separated data.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Leave multiplexing neutral.

questions.label: Demultiplexing status
questions.description: Indicates whether demultiplexing still needs to happen before downstream analysis.

options.label: Still needed
options.help: The pooled data are not yet separated.

options.label: Already done
options.help: Per-sample or per-barcode files already exist.

options.label: Not needed
options.help: Single-sample or already-separated input.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Leave demultiplexing neutral.

questions.label: Basecalling state or Dorado preference
questions.description: Used to insert or suppress basecalling steps and ONT notes.

options.label: Raw data, not basecalled yet
options.help: Basecalling still needs to happen.

options.label: Prefer FAST
options.help: Prioritize turnaround time.

options.label: Prefer HAC
options.help: Balanced accuracy and compute cost.

options.label: Prefer SUP
options.help: Highest accuracy and highest compute cost.

options.label: Already basecalled
options.help: Reads are already in FASTQ or BAM.

options.label: Not applicable
options.help: Use when no basecalling decision is relevant here.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Leave basecalling neutral.

questions.label: Adapter trimming and filtering
questions.description: Used to tune whether early cleaning steps are still needed.

options.label: Need trimming and filtering
options.help: Adapters and filtering are still pending.

options.label: Need trimming only
options.help: Adapters still need removal.

options.label: Need filtering only
options.help: Reads are trimmed but still need Q or length filtering.

options.label: Already trimmed and filtered
options.help: The data already passed through early read cleaning.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Leave preprocessing neutral.

questions.label: Are the long reads mostly short, with a median below 1000 bp?
questions.description: This can affect assembler preference in environmental long-read DNA workflows.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Median read length is below 1000 bp.

options.label: No
options.help: Median read length is 1000 bp or higher.

questions.label: Is this a very low-biomass environmental sample?
questions.description: Use this to add conservative interpretation notes for sparse environmental data.

options.label: Yes
options.help: The material is sparse or low-yield.

options.label: No
options.help: The material is not unusually sparse.

questions.label: Is genome assembly the main deliverable?
questions.description: Use this to emphasize assembly-centric interpretation.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Assembly and recovered genomes matter most.

options.label: No
options.help: Assembly is not the primary deliverable.

questions.label: Do you expect low viral load?
questions.description: Adds conservative notes for sparse RNA virome material.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Viral abundance is likely low.

options.label: No
options.help: Viral abundance is not expected to be especially low.

questions.label: Do you already have assembled contigs?
questions.description: Use this to suppress redundant assembly guidance when contigs already exist.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Contigs already exist.

options.label: No
options.help: Assembly still needs to happen.

questions.label: Is this a barcoded amplicon pool?
questions.description: Highlights demultiplexing and primer/barcode cleanup for amplicon workflows.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Barcoded amplicon pool.

options.label: No
options.help: Not a barcoded amplicon pool.

questions.label: Are Illumina support reads also available?
questions.description: Adds a note when paired-end short-read support exists alongside ONT 12S data.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Illumina support data are available.

options.label: No
options.help: Only one sequencing source is available.

questions.label: Are short reads available for hybrid assembly?
questions.description: Adds hybrid-assembly guidance when short-read support exists.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Short-read data are available.

options.label: No
options.help: Only long reads are available.

questions.label: Is a closed genome the priority?
questions.description: Adds finishing and circularization emphasis.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Closed or near-closed genome is the main deliverable.

options.label: No
options.help: Closed assembly is not the main priority.

questions.label: Are per-barcode isolates not yet split out?
questions.description: Adds comparative-isolate handling notes when the barcoded outputs are still pooled.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Per-barcode splitting still needs attention.

options.label: No
options.help: Per-barcode isolate outputs already exist.

questions.label: Is comparative phylogeny a main deliverable?
questions.description: Adds comparative isolate interpretation emphasis.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Comparative isolate outputs matter most.

options.label: No
options.help: Comparative phylogeny is secondary.

questions.label: Is host association the primary deliverable?
questions.description: Adds emphasis to methylation-aware host linkage outputs.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Host-association output is the main priority.

options.label: No
options.help: Host association is not the main priority.

questions.label: Is a matched control available?
questions.description: Adds AS-vs-control interpretation emphasis when matched control data exist.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Adaptive-sampling output can be compared against a control.

options.label: No
options.help: No matched control is available.

questions.label: Do the target reads already appear enriched?
questions.description: Adds a note when enrichment may already be sufficient before deeper recovery analysis.

options.label: Yes
options.help: The target looks enriched already.

options.label: No
options.help: Further enrichment-sensitive interpretation is still needed.

pages.title: Results
pages.summary: Review the recommended route, the selected published backend, and the expert-tuned action sheet.
